rationale: The citing paper references Ulkumen et al. (2010) in the theory section to support a specific claim: shopping-context categorization (narrow vs. broad) can induce a comparative evaluation mindset. This is used to motivate the broader premise that evaluation mode is situationally malleable, which is central to the citing paper's framework and experimental logic. The citation is brief, but it plays a clear conceptual support role rather than a purely perfunctory mention.
relation_description: The citing paper draws on the cited paper as prior evidence that category structure can shift consumers' evaluation mindset. This supports the citing paper's core setup that contextual cues can trigger comparative versus noncomparative evaluation in copycat-brand judgments.
